name: Healthcare Clinic
description: Patient enquiries, insurance checks and appointments for a private clinic, with an Appointments object and a pipeline that ends at discharge.
ai_context: You are a CRM assistant for a private healthcare clinic. Contacts are patients and referrers; a deal represents one episode of care, from first enquiry through consultation and treatment to discharge. Help with scheduling, insurance paperwork, referral follow-up and administrative reminders only. Never give clinical advice, never suggest a diagnosis, medication or treatment, and never invent a patient's medical history, test results, insurance authorisation or clinician availability — if it is not in the record, say so and ask the clinic.

automation_rules.name: Call a new enquiry back the next working day
automation_rules.description: Patients who wait more than a day for a callback usually book somewhere else. This queues the call and stamps the enquiry on the timeline.

params.title: Call {{contact.first_name}} {{contact.last_name}} — confirm reason for enquiry, payer and preferred clinician
New patient enquiry received
params.body: Enquiry logged for {{contact.first_name}}. Before booking: confirm date of birth, referral source, payer and policy number, and whether a GP or consultant referral letter is on file. Do not record clinical detail here — it belongs in the clinical system.

automation_rules.name: Tidy the paperwork whenever an episode moves stage
automation_rules.description: Every stage change is a moment where an appointment, an authorisation code or an invoice can be left hanging. This creates the admin check.

params.title: Admin check on {{deal.title}} — next appointment booked, pre-authorisation valid, fee estimate sent

kb_templates.company:
## About the clinic

[Clinic name] is a private [specialty, e.g. orthopaedic / dermatology / general practice] clinic in [location], established [year].

- Clinicians: [names and specialties]
- Sites: [addresses]
- Regulator / registration number: [e.g. CQC, HIQA, state board]

## Reception

Phone [number], email [address]. Opening hours [hours]. Out-of-hours enquiries go to [where].
kb_templates.products:
## Services and typical fees

| Service | Duration | Self-pay fee |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Initial consultation | [30 min] | [amount] |
| Follow-up | [15 min] | [amount] |
| [Procedure] | [time] | [amount] |

## Payers we are recognised by

[List insurers and scheme numbers.] Self-pay patients are quoted in writing before booking. Quote fees from this table only — never estimate a price that is not listed here.
kb_templates.playbook:
## Taking an enquiry

1. Capture name, date of birth, phone, email and how they heard about us.
2. Ask what they would like to be seen about — record it in the patient's own words, no interpretation.
3. Confirm payer: self-pay, insurer (name, policy number, pre-authorisation code) or scheme.
4. Ask whether a GP or consultant referral letter exists — some insurers will not settle without one.
5. Offer the first two available slots with the right clinician; do not hold a slot for more than 24 hours.

## Boundaries

Reception and CRM staff never triage symptoms, advise on urgency or discuss results. Anything clinical goes to a clinician the same day; anything urgent goes to emergency services.
kb_templates.process:
## From enquiry to discharge

1. **New Enquiry** — patient or referrer makes contact; capture details and reason for enquiry.
2. **Triage & Eligibility** — a clinician confirms we are the right service; payer, policy and referral letter checked.
3. **Consultation Booked** — slot confirmed in writing with fee estimate and cancellation policy.
4. **Consultation Attended** — patient seen; DNAs go back to New Enquiry for one rebooking attempt.
5. **Treatment Plan Proposed** — plan and costs sent, insurer pre-authorisation requested if needed.
6. **Treatment Underway** — appointments scheduled and invoiced as they happen.
7. **Discharged** — course of care complete, discharge letter sent to the patient and their GP.
8. **Did Not Proceed** — record the reason: cost, waiting time, insurer declined, referred elsewhere.
kb_templates.competitors:
## Where patients go instead

- The public system — free but slower; our honest advantage is waiting time and continuity with one named clinician.
- [Competing private clinic] — [their strength, their weakness].
- [Hospital outpatient department] — broader services, less personal.

Compete on speed to first appointment, transparent written pricing and the seniority of the clinician the patient actually sees. Never disparage another provider and never claim a clinical outcome.
